design_philosophy.core_loop: Real-time combat with power bar management, character switching, and Ring Command menu pauses. Party progresses through dungeon exploration, defeats enemies to gain experience and weapon orbs, upgrades weapons/magic at towns, advances story through map traversal.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing real-time action execution with tactical pause-menu decision-making. Managing three characters with distinct roles (attacker/healer/mage) and their individual strengths/weaknesses during combat.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a party of three diverse heroes across a vibrant high-fantasy world, mastering different weapons and elemental magic while cooperatively saving the world from an empire's conquest using an ancient flying fortress.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate design choice to use real-time combat instead of turn-based systems common in 1993 RPGs. Innovative Ring Command system that pauses action for decision-making rather than pure real-time play. Built-in cooperative multiplayer for up to 3 players with drop-in/drop-out mechanics and AI-controlled companions when solo.

difficulty.checkpointing: Town-based resting system allows hit point recovery and item purchase. Progression checkpoints at towns between dungeon segments.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Customizable AI settings for computer-controlled allies provide player control over companion behavior difficulty. Three-player cooperative mode reduces individual difficulty burden through shared combat load.

progression.upgrade_system: Dual upgrade system: Weapons increase skill levels (max 8) through repeated use, with each level unlocking a new special attack. Magic spells learned by rescuing eight Elementals representing different elements. Both weapon and magic have skill levels with increased effectiveness. Equipment can be purchased or upgraded by taking Weapon Orbs to blacksmiths in towns.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression through dungeon completion and Elemental rescue. Weapon Orbs dropped by defeating certain bosses and found in dungeons unlock weapon upgrades at blacksmith NPCs. Elementals unlock specific spell types when rescued.
replayability_hooks: Three-player cooperative multiplayer with drop-in/drop-out mechanics
Customizable AI settings for ally behavior
Character selection flexibility (can control any of three heroes at any time)
Eight weapon types with eight upgrade levels each and unique special attacks per level

world_building_techniques: High fantasy setting with distinct regions and towns
Character progression through three distinct heroes with individual roles and narrative arcs
NPC interactions in towns for story advancement and mechanical support
Environmental exploration of dungeons as story progression
Elemental spirits as world-building entities representing natural forces

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters tied to story progression and Weapon Orb drops. Certain bosses specifically reward weapon upgrade materials, creating mechanical significance for boss victories beyond progression gating.

faction_indicators: Empire attempting world conquest with ancient flying fortress as primary antagonistic force
Party of three heroes (protagonist, girl, sprite) as opposing faction
danger_signals: Enemy-infested countryside requiring traversal between destinations
Hostile creatures encountered during terrain navigation
environmental_storytelling: Cannon Travel Centers for expedited map traversal indicate infrastructure and civilization
Towns serve as safe zones with NPCs, blacksmiths, and story anchors
Dungeon architecture and enemy placement reflect world conflict and danger zones
Elemental spirit locations tied to specific geographical regions
